2026-04-16T05:45:00.4485642Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-16T05:45:00.4486094Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-16T05:45:00.4486474Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-16T05:45:00.4486792Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-16T05:45:00.4487037Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-16T05:45:00.4488560Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-16T05:48:28.8150494Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-16T05:46:03.943024571Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58208,\"title\":\"[ResourceGraph] Docs: Fill README gaps in Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit a6078e1 (PR #58068) by `@ArcturusZhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58068 added the brand-new `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` provisioning library (1.0.0-beta.1), introducing the `ResourceGraphQuery` resource type for declarative Azure Resource Graph shared-query provisioning via Bicep/azd. A CI fix was then applied in commit `a6078e1`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **\\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section is completely empty** — the README header exists but has no body text; it should explain that this library is used with `azd` for declarative infrastructure provisioning and does not require a credential object at configuration time\\n- **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section is too thin** — it gives one generic sentence but does not describe the primary type `ResourceGraphQuery`, its settable properties (`Query`, `Description`, `ETag`, `Tags`), the read-only outputs (`Id`, `ModifiedOn`, `ResultKind`), or how `BicepValue` / `ProvisioningParameter` work\\n- **\\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section is completely empty** — no links to related provisioning libraries, Azure Resource Graph documentation, or the Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) quickstart\\n- **No dedicated Samples test file** — the `#region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryBasic` snippet lives inside `BasicResourceGraphTests.cs` rather than a purpose-built `tests/Samples/Sample01_ResourceGraphQuery.cs` file; the canonical convention in this repo is a dedicated samples file per feature\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### 1a. Create a dedicated sample file\\n\\n**Create** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/tests/Samples/Sample01_ResourceGraphQuery.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph.Tests.Samples;\\n\\npublic class Sample01_ResourceGraphQuery\\n{\\n    [Test]\\n    [Description(\\u0026#34;Demonstrates creating a Resource Graph shared query (https://github.com/Azure/azure-quickstart-templates/tree/master/demos/resourcegraph-sharedquery-countos)\\u0026#34;)]\\n    public void CreateResourceGraphQuery()\\n    {\\n        #region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryBasic\\n        Infrastructure infra = new();\\n\\n        ProvisioningParameter queryCode = new(nameof(queryCode), typeof(string))\\n        {\\n            Description = \\u0026#34;The Azure Resource Graph query to be saved to the shared query.\\u0026#34;,\\n            Value = \\u0026#34;Resources | where type =~ \\u0026#39;Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines\\u0026#39; | summarize count() by tostring(properties.storageProfile.osDisk.osType)\\u0026#34;,\\n        };\\n        infra.Add(queryCode);\\n\\n        ProvisioningParameter queryDescription = new(nameof(queryDescription), typeof(string))\\n        {\\n            Description = \\u0026#34;The description of the saved Azure Resource Graph query.\\u0026#34;,\\n            Value = \\u0026#34;This shared query counts all virtual machine resources and summarizes by the OS type.\\u0026#34;,\\n        };\\n        infra.Add(queryDescription);\\n\\n        ResourceGraphQuery query =\\n            new(nameof(query))\\n            {\\n                Query = queryCode,\\n                Description = queryDescription,\\n            };\\n        infra.Add(query);\\n        #endregion\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** Once this file is created, remove the duplicate `#region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryBasic` / `#endregion` block from `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/tests/BasicResourceGraphTests.cs` and replace it with the plain code (no region tags) so there are no duplicate snippet names.\\n\\n#### 1b. Edit the README\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md`\\n\\nReplace the empty `### Authenticate the Client` section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\n`Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` is a **declarative provisioning library** used to define Azure Resource Graph infrastructure in C#. It generates [Bicep]((aka.ms/redacted) templates which are then deployed by the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((aka.ms/redacted) No credential object is required when using this library — authentication is handled by `azd` at deployment time.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the thin `## Key concepts` section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n`Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` uses the **Azure Provisioning** programming model where you describe Azure resources in C# and the library generates Bicep templates.\\n\\n### ResourceGraphQuery\\n\\n`ResourceGraphQuery` represents an [Azure Resource Graph shared query]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) (`Microsoft.ResourceGraph/queries`). Key properties:\\n\\n| Property | Type | Description |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `Name` | `BicepValue` | The name of the query resource (required). |\\n| `Location` | `BicepValue` | The Azure region (required). |\\n| `Query` | `BicepValue` | The KQL query string. |\\n| `Description` | `BicepValue` | A human-readable description. |\\n| `ETag` | `BicepValue` | Used for optimistic concurrency. |\\n| `Tags` | `BicepDictionary` | Resource tags. |\\n| `Id` _(output)_ | `BicepValue` | The fully-qualified resource ID after deployment. |\\n| `ModifiedOn` _(output)_ | `BicepValue` | Last modification time. |\\n| `ResultKind` _(output)_ | `BicepValue` | The query result type. |\\n\\n### BicepValue and ProvisioningParameter\\n\\nProperties accept either literal values or `ProvisioningParameter` references. A `ProvisioningParameter` becomes a Bicep `param` declaration, allowing callers to supply values at deployment time.\\n\\n### Infrastructure\\n\\nThe `Infrastructure` class is the root container. Add all resources and parameters to it; then call `Build()` to get the generated Bicep or pass it to `azd` for deployment.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the empty `## Next steps` section with:\\n\\n````markdown\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- [Azure Resource Graph documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- [Azure Resource Graph query language reference]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- Other `Azure.Provisioning.*` libraries: [Azure.Provisioning]((www.nuget.org/redacted), [Azure.Provisioning.Resources]((www.nuget.org/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe snippet `ResourceGraphQueryBasic` is already referenced in the README with the correct placeholder:\\n\\n````\\n````\\n```\\n````\\n\\nAfter creating `Sample01_ResourceGraphQuery.cs` with the `#region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryBasic` block and **removing** the duplicate region from `BasicResourceGraphTests.cs`, run `Update-Snippets.ps1` (Step 4 below) to inject the code into the README.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nAll six required sections will be present after the edits above:\\n1. ✅ Getting started — Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the Client (needs content — see Step 1b)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts — needs expansion (see Step 1b)\\n3. ✅ Examples — already has `ResourceGraphQuery` example\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting — present\\n5. ✅ Next steps — needs content (see Step 1b)\\n6. ✅ Contributing — present\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 resourcegraph` — injects snippet code from the new sample file into the README placeholder\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 resourcegraph` — regenerates public API listings (no new public types were added but good practice for a new library)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update A CI fix was then applied in commit `a6078e1`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **\\\"Authenticate the Client\\\" section is completely empty** — the README header exists but has no body text; it should explain that this library is used with `azd` for declarative infrastructure provisioning and does not require a credential object at configuration time\\n- **\\\"Key concepts\\\" section is too thin** — it gives one generic sentence but does not describe the primary type `ResourceGraphQuery`, its settable properties (`Query`, `Description`, `ETag`, `Tags`), the read-only outputs (`Id`, `ModifiedOn`, `ResultKind`), or how `BicepValue\\u003cT\\u003e` / `ProvisioningParameter` work\\n- **\\\"Next steps\\\" section is completely empty** — no links to related provisioning libraries, Azure Resource Graph documentation, or the Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) quickstart\\n- **No dedicated Samples test file** — the `#region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryBasic` snippet lives inside `BasicResourceGraphTests.cs` rather than a purpose-built `tests/Samples/Sample01_ResourceGraphQuery.cs` file; the canonical convention in this repo is a dedicated samples file per feature\\n\\n\\u003cdetails\\u003e\\n\\u003csummary\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\u003c/summary\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### 1a. Create a dedicated sample file\\n\\n**Create** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/tests/Samples/Sample01_ResourceGraphQuery.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph.Tests.Samples;\\n\\npublic class Sample01_ResourceGraphQuery\\n{\\n    [Test]\\n    [Description(\\\"Demonstrates creating a Resource Graph shared query (https://github.com/Azure/azure-quickstart-templates/tree/master/demos/resourcegraph-sharedquery-countos)\\\")]\\n    public void CreateResourceGraphQuery()\\n    {\\n        #region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryBasic\\n        Infrastructure infra = new();\\n\\n        ProvisioningParameter queryCode = new(nameof(queryCode), typeof(string))\\n        {\\n            Description = \\\"The Azure Resource Graph query to be saved to the shared query.\\\",\\n            Value = \\\"Resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines' | summarize count() by tostring(properties.storageProfile.osDisk.osType)\\\",\\n        };\\n        infra.Add(queryCode);\\n\\n        ProvisioningParameter queryDescription = new(nameof(queryDescription), typeof(string))\\n        {\\n            Description = \\\"The description of the saved Azure Resource Graph query.\\\",\\n            Value = \\\"This shared query counts all virtual machine resources and summarizes by the OS type.\\\",\\n        };\\n        infra.Add(queryDescription);\\n\\n        ResourceGraphQuery query =\\n            new(nameof(query))\\n            {\\n                Query = queryCode,\\n                Description = queryDescription,\\n            };\\n        infra.Add(query);\\n        #endregion\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n\\u003e **Note:** Once this file is created, remove the duplicate `#region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryBasic` / `#endregion` block from `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/tests/BasicResourceGraphTests.cs` and replace it with the plain code (no region tags) so there are no duplicate snippet names.\\n\\n#### 1b. Edit the README\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md`\\n\\nReplace the empty `### Authenticate the Client` section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\n`Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` is a **declarative provisioning library** used to define Azure Resource Graph infrastructure in C#. It generates [Bicep]((aka.ms/redacted) templates which are then deployed by the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((aka.ms/redacted) No credential object is required when using this library — authentication is handled by `azd` at deployment time.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the thin `## Key concepts` section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n`Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` uses the **Azure Provisioning** programming model where you describe Azure resources in C# and the library generates Bicep templates.\\n\\n### ResourceGraphQuery\\n\\n`ResourceGraphQuery` represents an [Azure Resource Graph shared query]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) (`Microsoft.ResourceGraph/queries`). Key properties:\\n\\n| Property | Type | Description |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `Name` | `BicepValue\\u003cstring\\u003e` | The name of the query resource (required). |\\n| `Location` | `BicepValue\\u003cAzureLocation\\u003e` | The Azure region (required). |\\n| `Query` | `BicepValue\\u003cstring\\u003e` | The KQL query string. |\\n| `Description` | `BicepValue\\u003cstring\\u003e` | A human-readable description. |\\n| `ETag` | `BicepValue\\u003cETag\\u003e` | Used for optimistic concurrency. |\\n| `Tags` | `BicepDictionary\\u003cstring\\u003e` | Resource tags. |\\n| `Id` _(output)_ | `BicepValue\\u003cResourceIdentifier\\u003e` | The fully-qualified resource ID after deployment. |\\n| `ModifiedOn` _(output)_ | `BicepValue\\u003cDateTimeOffset\\u003e` | Last modification time. |\\n| `ResultKind` _(output)_ | `BicepValue\\u003cResultKind\\u003e` | The query result type. |\\n\\n### BicepValue and ProvisioningParameter\\n\\nProperties accept either literal values or `ProvisioningParameter` references. A `ProvisioningParameter` becomes a Bicep `param` declaration, allowing callers to supply values at deployment time.\\n\\n### Infrastructure\\n\\nThe `Infrastructure` class is the root container. Add all resources and parameters to it; then call `Build()` to get the generated Bicep or pass it to `azd` for deployment.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the empty `## Next steps` section with:\\n\\n````markdown\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- [Azure Resource Graph documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- [Azure Resource Graph query language reference]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- Other `Azure.Provisioning.*` libraries: [Azure.Provisioning]((www.nuget.org/redacted), [Azure.Provisioning.Resources]((www.nuget.org/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe snippet `ResourceGraphQueryBasic` is already referenced in the README with the correct placeholder:\\n\\n````\\n````C# Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryBasic\\n```\\n````\\n\\nAfter creating `Sample01_ResourceGraphQuery.cs` with the `#region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryBasic` block and **removing** the duplicate region from `BasicResourceGraphTests.cs`, run `Update-Snippets.ps1` (Step 4 below) to inject the code into the README.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nAll six required sections will be present after the edits above:\\n1. ✅ Getting started — Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the Client (needs content — see Step 1b)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts — needs expansion (see Step 1b)\\n3. ✅ Examples — already has `ResourceGraphQuery` example\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting — present\\n5. ✅ Next steps — needs content (see Step 1b)\\n6. ✅ Contributing — present\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 resourcegraph` — injects snippet code from the new sample file into the README placeholder\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 resourcegraph` — regenerates public API listings (no new public types were added but good practice for a new library)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`\\n\\n\\u003c/details\\u003e\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u003e [!TIP]\\n\\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to
